semgrep.description: GLOBAL — Security scanning for AI-generated code. Semgrep detects vulnerabilities, secrets, and supply chain risks. Always-on because security is non-negotiable in every project.

sequential-thinking.description: GLOBAL — Structured problem-solving for complex reasoning. Breaks architectural decisions and large-scale refactoring into logical steps. Supports the CTO Strategy Layer.

playwright.description: RECOMMENDED — Browser automation and E2E testing. Maps to e2e-testing skill. AI Validation Pyramid Layer 3 (E2E). Auto-provisioned when playwright is detected.

eslint.description: RECOMMENDED — Code quality and linting via MCP. AI Validation Pyramid Layer 1 (static analysis). Auto-provisioned when eslint is detected in project.

postgres.description: RECOMMENDED — Read-only PostgreSQL schema inspection and queries. Batuta core stack. Requires DATABASE_URI env var. Auto-provisioned when postgres is detected.

hooks.prompt:
BATUTA BOOTSTRAP — THE RULE: If a skill applies to your current task, you MUST invoke it. No exceptions. If an MCP server is available and relevant, you MUST consult it before relying on training data.

Your available skills were auto-discovered and listed in the Batuta Skill Inventory injected above. Review them before proceeding.

Red-flag rationalizations — if you catch yourself thinking any of these, STOP and load the relevant skill:
- "This is just something simple" — Simple tasks still need team conventions.
- "I already know how to do this" — Skills encode the TEAM way, not just knowledge.
- "I need more context first" — Skill loading PRECEDES context gathering.
- "The skill is overkill for this" — The skill defines minimum quality. Follow it.

Respond with {"ok": true}.

ALWAYS do both steps before stopping, in this order:

STEP 1 — Write .batuta/CHECKPOINT.md (mandatory, no exceptions, even if no work was done):
Write the file with exactly this structure:

# Checkpoint — [current ISO timestamp]

## Qué estoy haciendo
[Current task in one sentence — or "Sin tarea activa" if nothing was being done]

## Estado
- Paso actual: [N de M steps, or "N/A"]
- Archivo/módulo en trabajo: [path or "N/A"]
- Branch: [current git branch or "N/A"]

## Intentos y resultados
[List what you tried and what happened. "Sin intentos fallidos" if none.]

## Decisiones tomadas (con evidencia)
[List decisions with evidence. "Sin decisiones pendientes" if none.]

## Qué falta
[Pending steps as checkboxes. "Nada pendiente" if done.]

## Gotchas descubiertos
[Verified facts with the evidence that confirmed them. "Ninguno" if none.]

STEP 2 — Update .batuta/session.md (only if significant work):
If you performed significant work (completed SDD phase, modified 3+ files, resolved a bug, or had 5+ exchanges), update .batuta/session.md following the Session Budget (80 lines max). If no significant work, skip.

Respond with {"ok": true} after completing both steps.
